To the rear, the high fence enclosed garden offers a generous sized patio area providing the ideal space for outside dining and entertaining plus an area of lawn and a useful storage shed. Other benefits include convenient internal lift providing effortless access between floors, under-floor heating and solar panels. Locally, the property is set within easy reach of a variety of well-regarded schools including Cheam Park Farm Primary Academy, St Ceciliaâs Catholic Primary School and Cheam High School. North Cheam provides an array of shops, restaurants, supermarkets and amenities whilst Worcester Park and Cheam Village are a short drive from the property. Commuters will benefit from West Sutton train station approx. 1mile walk as well as a variety of bus routes towards Kingston, Sutton and Morden with itâs Northern Line tube

description: *** no onward chain *** Located on Windsor Avenue in the charming area of Cheam, this end of terraced house is a stunning example of modern living. The use of premium materials and high-quality fittings throughout ensures a luxurious feel, while the incorporation of an air source heat pump system, complete with a 7-year extended warranty, highlights its energy-efficient credentials. This feature not only promotes environmentally conscious living but also promises economical heating for years to come. Additionally, West Sutton railway station is just a short distance away, offering easy access to London and the surrounding areas. Offered to the market chain free, this exceptional home presents a rare opportunity to acquire a turn-key property that has been finished to perfection. Do not miss the chance to make this beautiful house your new home. Accomodation Front Exterior The property is approached via a gravel driveway enclosed by a charming picket fence, with a stone tiled doorstep leading to a country mouse grey UPVC front entrance door with high-security multifunctional locking system, featuring decorative glass panelling. There is also an external cold-water tap and an outdoor electrical point and lighting. Entrance Hall The entrance hall features attractive bevel line hard wearing rich oak-effect flooring and a decorative wooden panelled feature wall with a mirror positioned to the left-hand side. A staircase with wooden balustrades and a carpeted runner leads to the first floor. There are three useful storage cupboards, one of which houses the Samsung Air Source Heat Pump with an impressive energy rating of A+++. Ample downlighting and underfloor heating. Situated in the heart of the sought-after Dover House Conservation Area, this charming end-of-terrace home on Parkstead Road offers a beautifully balanced blend of classic architecture and contemporary living. Upon entering the house, a welcoming hallway leads to an impressive open-plan living space that forms the heart of the home. The beautifully appointed modern kitchen enjoys elevated views across the green to the front and beyond towards the city. This expansive area comfortably accommodates a large dining table, a discreet home office nook, and a relaxed seating area that overlooks the south-facing rear garden. Rare for the area, this generous room is flooded with natural light and enjoys views across the wrap-around garden, offering a versatile additional living or entertaining space. One of the standout features of the property is its exceptional outdoor offering. The home benefits from three separate garden areas, ensuring sunlight can be enjoyed throughout the day. The property also benefits from a large standalone garage with two entrances, offering flexible use for storage, a workshop, or studio space. Upstairs, the property offers three well-proportioned double bedrooms with excellent storage options, alongside a smaller room currently arranged as a walk-in wardrobe, which could equally function as a home office or nursery. There is also potential to extend into the loft (subject to the usual planning permissions), as several neighbouring properties have done, creating the opportunity for an additional bedroom and en-suite. Parkstead Road is ideally located within the charming Dover House Conservation Area, known for its attractive architecture and strong sense of community. The property benefits from excellent transport links and convenient access to a range of amenities, including Barnes Station, Putney High Street, East Putney Underground, and Putney mainline station. Nearby green spaces such as Barnes Common, Putney Heath, and Richmond Park provide wonderful opportunities for outdoor recreation. This exceptional property presents a rare opportunity to acquire a spacious and characterful home in Putney, offering a unique combination of generous living space, extensive gardens, parking, and future potential-features seldom found together in the area. The Personal Agent are delighted to welcome to the market this stunning modern three bedroom semi detached home set on one of Stoneleigh's most desirable road minutes form both Stoneleigh Broadway and Train Station. The property itself boasts a bright and spacious lounge dining room which flows through into a modern kitchen with a separate utility room. While back round to the hallway there is access to a downstairs shower room. To the first floor there are three bedrooms and a modern family bathroom with access to a loft ideal for storage storage. To the rear of the property there is a landscaped and private garden. This highly energy-efficient home is equipped with a Joule Module Air E exhaust air heat pump and Mechanical Heat Recovery Ventilation (mhrv) system. It reuses warm air from everyday household activities to heat both the property and water, delivering exceptional energy performance (up to 475% heating efficiency). The system runs automatically with built-in filters to reduce dust and pollution, while a central manifold system keeps all pipework accessible and maintenance-friendly. Benefits include lower utility bills, no condensation or mould, and a consistently warm, draught-free home. Additional features include four roof-mounted solar panels with app control, a 7kW EV charger in the porch, and ultra-fast Full Fibre to the Premises (FTTP) broadband. The kitchen boasts fingerprint-resistant Italian-finished cabinetry and premium Vado brassware. A remote-controlled extractor works alongside the mhrv system for clean air, and a Texecom smart alarm system allows for remote monitoring-ideal for peace of mind while away. Stoneleigh is a highly sought after residential area with a bustling Broadway of shops and restaurants at its heart, as well as a mainline railway station with services to Waterloo every twenty minutes. property_name: 3 bed link detached house for sale
description: Enjoying a secluded location in Thamesgate Close, a quiet residential cul de sac, this handsome three bedroom, two bathroom link detached house is moments from the green spaces of the Ham Lands Local Nature Reserve and the path to the Thames riverside at Teddington Lock, with its pedestrian/cycle bridge over to Teddington High Street. The downstairs space comprises a front hallway, cloakroom, good sized reception, well appointed kitchen, dining room and southeast facing conservatory, from which French doors lead out into a substantial garden. A side door permits access to the generously sized garage, which could be used as habitable space. Upstairs there are three bedrooms, including a large master bedroom with two broad windows and en suite bathroom, as well as a separate family bathroom. For anyone seeking a compact, well laid out house in a tranquil location, surrounded by nature but within easy reach of local amenities, this might just be the end of your search.

Situated in the heart of the peaceful and highly sought-after village of Ham, this attractive three-bedroom semi-detached home is presented in good condition and offers well-balanced accommodation with an excellent flow between living and bedroom space. Currently arranged over two floors, the ground floor features a generous reception room with attractive wood flooring and a charming feature fireplace, creating a warm and welcoming focal point to the home. The reception space flows naturally through to a bright and airy sunroom, providing an ideal setting for relaxation, dining, or evening entertaining while enjoying views over the garden. The property also benefits from a well-maintained, galley-style, fully equipped kitchen, thoughtfully designed to maximise functionality and storage. Adjacent to the kitchen is a separate utility room offering excellent additional storage and practical laundry space, along with direct access to the mature rear garden. Upstairs, the house features three well-proportioned bedrooms and a family bathroom, offering comfortable space for a growing family or those needing a dedicated home office. Outside, the property boasts a good-sized, south-facing garden-a highly coveted feature that ensures maximum sunlight throughout the day. This private and established outdoor environment is perfect for gardening, entertaining, or family use. The property further benefits from a private driveway providing valuable off-street parking for multiple vehicles. In addition, the house offers substantial scope to extend in several areas (subject to the usual planning consents), providing an exciting opportunity for incoming owners to further enhance both the living accommodation and the long-term value of the property. Ideally positioned within the catchment area for highly regarded local schools-including the âOutstandingâ Grey Court School-Ham enjoys a unique village atmosphere while remaining perfectly located between the vibrant and historic towns of Richmond and Kingston. Both offer an extensive selection of shops, restaurants, cafÃ©s, and amenities, together with fast rail links providing easy access into Central London and beyond. One of the propertyâs standout features is its proximity to the River Thames; a scenic 10-minute stroll leads you directly to the iconic Teddington Lock and its footbridge, offering a picturesque pedestrian gateway to Teddingtonâs independent boutiques and riverside pubs and cafes. The property is also just a short stroll from the picturesque Ham Common and the world-renowned Richmond Park with Ham Gate located nearby, residents enjoy effortless access to over 2,500 acres of Royal parkland, offering vast open green spaces, scenic walking and cycling routes, and an abundance of wildlife right on your doorstep. description: Mark Anthony are pleased to market this impressive and well presented town house within a private gated mews in the heart of Ewell Village. The location is ideal for all aspects of village life with its local shops, restaurants and Ewell Grove, Glyn, St Clement's and Ewell Castle Schools. Our clients purchased the property new from renowned builder Try Homes in 2004 and have since upgraded the property to include replacement windows, a new Vaillant central heating boiler and EV charger. The spacious accommodation comprises of a grand and welcoming reception hall, leading through to a front aspect reception room, utility room, downstairs cloakroom, eat in kitchen diner open to a conservatory overlooking a well loved and maintained garden. The kitchen is fitted with shaker style units, granite work surfaces and a range style cooker and hood. The kitchen area also features an open fireplace and ceramic tiled flooring. On the first floor there is a spacious landing, formal sitting room and master bedroom suite. The sitting room features a gas coal effect fireplace and Juliette balcony with pleasant views. The Master bedroom suite features a balcony, built in wardrobes and en suite bathroom with and separate shower cubicle. On the second floor there is a guest bedroom suite, with built in wardrobes and en suite shower room. There are two further bedrooms and family bathroom. Outside to the rear, there is a landscaped rear garden with a paved terrace. To the front, the property is approached via electric gates into a mews courtyard with under croft parking and garage. Internal viewing is recommended to appreciate this splendid home set in a gated mews in the heart of Ewell Village

There is also a loft for storage with loft ladder, lighting which is boarded and fully insulated with two Velux windows where you can enjoy wonderful views and is an ideal area to watch fireworks on fireworks night. Deceptively spacious, the property comprises; an entrance hallway leading to a living room with a conersvatory/lean to, a kitchen/breakfast room and three bedrooms with bathroom. The kitchen leads to an L shaped covered area with separate storage cupboard. There is also the benefit of a large 22ft loft with front aspect window which hasnât been converted but does offer the scope for a further two bedrooms. Externally the rear garden is south easterly facing, an impressive circa 233ft and is mainly laid to lawn with many mature shrubs and trees. The rear garden also has a large pond and a detached brick built workshop. Elmbridge Borough Council: Band E (EPC rating: D). The property is located within easy reach of primary schools, shops, Hersham train station (London Waterloo circa 28 mins) and the River Thames with towpath beside which leads up to Hurst Park recreational fields, Hampton Court with its restaurants, Palace and train station and Walton on Thames with its busy high street shops. There are recreational parks closeby, including Grovelands Park along with wonderful walks along Molesey Heath. The property is also within easy reach of local supermarkets, Pavilion gym with tennis court and swimming pool facilities, East Molesey cricket club, Molesey Rowing club, Molesey sailing club, Molesey football club and Hurst swimming poo

description: An attractive three bedroom semi detached house close to Richmond Park. Description This charming semi detached house was extended to the rear on both the ground/first floor and into the loft. The property benefits from new flooring and high end appliances throughout. The house is set back from the road behind a pretty front garden. To the front is a welcoming reception room with good natural light which flows through to an impressive modern kitchen/dining space. Kingston town centre is within one mile approximately, offering a selection of everyday shops and restaurants with access onto the River Thames. Norbiton rail station for London is within 650 metres (London Waterloo in approximately 25 minutes) and there is good access to the A3 Kingston Hill. A stunning 3 bedroom house in turnkey condition, near St Margarets station, Moormead Park and many sought-after local schools. This beautifully designed and finished 3-bedroom house has been updated to a very high standard, offering a turnkey family home for people looking to move to the increasingly popular St Margarets area. The ground floor offers a superb open-plan living area with wooden flooring throughout that retains a cosy feel, making it perfect for family living with clear zones for cooking, dining and relaxing, while also offering a fabulous entertaining space. address: Penny House, 29 Wolsey Road, Esher, Surrey KT10
description: Immaculately presented detached period home in the heart of Esher. Description This beautifully presented detached three bedroom home is positioned in a fantastic residential road which conveniently leads directly onto Esher high street. The property boasts a wealth of period charm with sash windows, elegant architraves and cornicing. High ceilings throughout enable natural light to flood into the property, creating a bright and spacious living environment. The attractive entrance hall with traditional arch leads to the reception room at the front of the property. This generous room features an impressive bay window and an original working fireplace provides the perfect focal point. The fantastic open plan kitchen/dining room is located to the rear of the property and benefits from direct garden access via a patio door. The kitchen is fitted with a range of traditional white style shaker style wall and base mounted cabinetry, grey worktops and integrated appliances including a gas hob, oven, dishwasher, fridge/freezer, wine fridge, microwave, washer and dryer. The tiled stone flooring throughout this room benefits from underfloor heating. Completing the ground floor accommodation is a guest WC. On the first floor there are two bedrooms and two bathrooms. The well-proportioned principal bedroom features a wall of bespoke fitted wardrobes. Adjacent is a luxurious family bathroom with free standing bath. Bedroom 2 enjoys views over the rear garden and is currently set up as a secondary reception space. A modern shower room adjacent serves this room and the top floor bedroom. Bedroom 3 can be found on the second floor and offers a dressing room and WC. This charming space would make a lovely childrenâs bedroom with play area. Externally the property has a pretty landscaped rear garden. A terraced area provides the perfect spot for al fresco dining and entertaining. This leads onto a lawned area bordered by mature flower beds. A second seating area to the rear of the garden, makes the most of the evening sun. To the side of the property is a further terraced area, providing another space to enjoy the stunning garden. If required, prospective buyers could extend the property into this space (STPP). A useful storage shed can be found at the end of the garden. Parking is available in the road to residents, with permits available via Elmbridge council. Location Wolsey Road is perfectly positioned close to Esher Green and the High Street which offers a great range of restaurants, bars, coffee shops, an Everyman cinema and a Waitrose supermarket. Esher train station provides regular and direct trains to London Waterloo in around 23 minutes. Esher is the perfect choice for those in search of nature, space and quality of life. The surrounding woods and commons provide a green backdrop ideal for exploring the range of sports clubs and leisure venues, including Esher Cricket Club and Sandown Park racecourse. The nearby A3 provides direct road access to central London and the Surrey countryside, Heathrow and Gatwick airports. The Elmbridge Borough is popular with families as it has an impressive array of both independent and state schools. Square Footage: 1,536 sq f

description: An exceptional fully renovated four-bedroom semi-detached family residence with private garden, ideally positioned on the desirable outskirts of Kingston. Stylish contemporary interiors blend seamlessly with high-quality finishes throughout, creating a sophisticated yet highly practical home perfectly suited to modern family living. To the front, an elegant reception room with a bay window provides a comfortable setting for both relaxation and entertaining. To the rear, the house opens into a superb open-plan kitchen and breakfast room forming the heart of the home. Designed with both style and practicality in mind, the space enjoys excellent natural light from overhead skylights and wide sliding doors that lead directly onto the terrace and garden beyond. The kitchen is fitted with a comprehensive range of bespoke wall and base cabinetry, complemented by quality work surfaces and a central island with breakfast bar seating. Integrated appliances, an inset sink and range-style cooker complete the space, while a separate utility room provides additional storage and appliance space. EPC Rating: E Location Discover Worcester Park â A Commuter Favourite with a Village Feel Worcester Park is a popular and well-connected suburban area on the SurreyâSouth West London border, offering a perfect mix of convenience, community, and green space. Just 10 miles from central London, the area is ideal for commuters, with Worcester Park Station providing direct trains to London Waterloo in under 30 minutes. description: Situated on the popular Aragon Road in North Kingston, this impressive end-terrace 1930s house offers a perfect blend of classic charm and modern convenience. A central island unit and a range cooker provide the perfect setting for cooking and baking for family and friends. Bi folding doors lead directly onto a pretty rear garden. Conveniently situated on a tree-lined avenue, this charming property enjoys easy access to outstanding local primary schools such as Thomson House and Sheen Mount, and excellent transport links, with Mortlake station (London - Waterloo) only 8 minutes walk away (according to Google maps). address: 11 Hansler Grove, East Molesey, Surrey KT8
description: Contemporary home with wonderful garden in sought-after location. Description This property has a wonderfully individual character, shaped by its origins and long family ownership. Built in 1969 by Mr Biss, a well known local architect, it carries the hallmarks of thoughtful mid century design: Generous proportions, an eclectic layout and beautiful wooden doors imported from Asia that give the interior a warm, crafted feel. Having remained in the same family for over fifty years, it comes to the market with no onwards chain. The galleried hallway provides an impressive, light-filled entrance. To the right is a generous dining room with large front aspect window and an open connection to the reception room behind. To the other side of the entrance hall is the kitchen, which offers plenty of built in storage and a number of integrated appliances. The lower ground floor provides a practical and versatile foundation to the home. A spacious laundry room sits alongside a study, both of which could easily be used as a cosy cinema, gaming space or gym if required. The principal reception room can be found on the raised ground floor. Stretching generously across the rear of the property, this room creates an inviting setting for family life and entertaining, with sliding doors opening onto the rear garden. A connecting door from the reception room leads through to an adjoining store room which is unusually large and adaptable. The garage, accessed via stairs from the store, houses the boiler and provides external access to the garden via a rear door. The upper floors offer a well balanced arrangement of bedrooms. The rear aspect principal bedroom includes fitted wardrobes and an ensuite bathroom with bath and separate shower. There are three further bedrooms, one with a wall of fitted wardrobes, in addition to the family bathroom with the convenience of twin sinks. Outside, the garden is a standout feature-mature, private, and beautifully established. A covered outdoor kitchen area makes it perfect for relaxed gatherings and summer entertaining. The property also benefits from planning permission (ref: 2017/1799) for a first floor side extension and an additional front rooflight, offering clear potential for future expansion while respecting the homeâs original design. Please note the property has been virtually staged to show how some of the rooms can be utilised. Location Hansler Grove is a sought after residential road in the heart of East Molesey, conveniently located for the amenities of Walton Road and Bridge Road, both providing a mix of restaurants, coffee shops and boutiques. Kingston-upon-Thames is nearby (2.4 miles) and provides a wider selection of shopping. Hampton Court Station is just half a mile away and provides regular and direct trains into London Waterloo in around 33 minutes. Hampton Court Palace (0.7 miles) and Bushy Park (1 mile) are also easily accessible for days out and there is an excellent choice of both state and independent schools in the area, making it ideal for families. The M25 (10 miles) provides access to both Heathrow and Gatwick airports. Please note all distances and times are approximate

A home of rare scale and versatility, this substantial five bedroom semi detached property sits proudly on a larger than average plot, offering the space, privacy and flexibility that modern family life demands. Incorporating an impressive two bedroom split level annex, generous living areas and an expansive garden, it is perfectly suited to multi-generational living, blended families, or those seeking a home that can evolve with them over time. From the moment you step into the central hallway, the sense of space is unmistakable. To the front, the bay fronted living room provides a calm, elegant retreat, a place to unwind at the end of the day or gather for family nights in. To the rear, the house opens into a beautifully connected kitchen/dining/family space that forms the true heart of the home. The fully fitted kitchen flows into a generous dining area and onward to a relaxed family room, all overlooking the garden. This is a space made for hosting, for everyday bustle, for celebrations that spill out onto the terrace on warm summer evenings. Upstairs, the main house offers three generous bedrooms and a stylish family bathroom, each room thoughtfully proportioned to accommodate the needs of a growing household and there is direct access to annex side of the home which provides two further bedrooms and a second bathroom. The split level Annex offers Independence, flexibility and possibility and is where this home truly stands apart. With its own entrance, fitted kitchen, lounge/dining area and direct access to the garden, it offers complete independence while remaining connected to the main house. For those working from home, it provides the perfect professional environment, equally suited to childminders, therapists, hairdressers or anyone seeking a dedicated workspace separate from the main household. Upstairs, the annex offers two versatile rooms and a family bathroom to create a self-contained living space ideal for grandparents, older children, visiting family or an au pair. This is multi generational living at its best: Privacy without distance, independence without separation. Outside the expansive rear garden offers a wonderful sense of openness, with plenty of room for children to play, explore and enjoy the outdoors. For keen gardeners, it is a blank canvas ready to be shaped into something special. A large terrace provides the perfect setting for outdoor dining, weekend barbecues and long summer afternoons. Side access leads directly to the annex, adding convenience and privacy. Stoneleigh is a highly sought after residential area with a bustling Broadway of shops and restaurants at its heart, as well as a mainline railway station with services to Waterloo every twenty minutes. There is a choice of infant and junior schools within the immediate vicinity and the Historic Nonsuch Park runs alongside the South East side. Both the M25 and A3 are easily accessible giving a straight forward route to London and both Heathrow and Gatwick international airports. Tenure - Freehold Council tax band -

address: Milestone Cottage, 26 Milbourne Lane, Esher, Surrey KT10
description: Attractive period home with versatile accommodation. property_name: 4 bed end terrace house for sale
description: Beautifully presented townhouse situated within this sought after development next to scenic Royal Bushy Park. Description Admiralty Way was built approximately 2001 by Laing Homes, built on land that was formerly part of the National Physical Laboratory, recalling the work of the Admiralty Research Laboratory, hence the road's name. This particular house was built latterly by the developer in 2007 and stands as an imposing end of terrace home arranged over three floors, enjoying open views towards the expansive green spaces of the second largest of London's Royal Parks, Bushy Park. The ground floor comprises the living room to the front with modern and stylish kitchen/dining to the rear, with the addition of a WC off the welcoming entrance hall. To the first floor, there are two double bedrooms; the rear bedroom benefitting from far-reaching views towards the ancient woodlands of Bushy Park and a balcony offering the same, together with a family bathroom. The top floor comprises the principal bedroom with ensuite and again, open views towards the park with Juliette balcony, and a further double bedroom with balcony to the front. To the rear, there is a pretty and functional wall-enclosed garden laid to lawn with patio which enjoys a south facing orientation. The communal land to the front of the house, provides further green space for amenity and enjoyment and with limited traffic, can provide a play area for children. The house benefits from side access and the exclusive use of two side-by-side parking spaces. There is a nominal cost of Â£600 p.a. Towards the upkeep of the private road and associated communal amenity, run by the residentâs association. In accordance with the Estate Agents Act 1979 (section 21), we wish to inform prospective buyers of this property that the seller is connected to an employee of Savills. Location Admiralty Way is located off Queens Road, 0.3 miles from Teddington mainline station which is served by South Western Railway and provides services to London Waterloo and Wimbledon. Local bus services run from Broad Street, which offer links to Richmond, the popular shopping town of Kingston and provide a direct line to Heathrow Airport. Broad Street offers supermarkets, boutique shops, and independent restaurants. Bushy Park is approximately 0.3 miles from the property. The park is the second largest of London's Royal Parks at 445 hectares. The park offers woodlands and playgrounds to explore. The park is also home to many local sports clubs. There are also many entertainment venues including the Landmark Centre and the prestigious Lensbury Club. -Full Description- This outstanding property is arranged over three floors and has been finished to an exceptional standard, offering a stylish and highly functional layout suited to modern living. The ground floor is centred around a superb open-plan kitchen, living, and dining area, creating a bright and sociable heart of the home. The contemporary kitchen is complemented by sleek finishes and ample storage. Additional ground floor spaces include two office/reception rooms, a utility room, a cloakroom, and a W.C., providing excellent flexibility for home working or family needs. The first floor offers three well-proportioned bedrooms, including a spacious principal bedroom with an en-suite, along with a modern family bathroom and landing area. On the second floor, an additional large bedroom suite benefits from its own shower room and adjoining dressing/storage space, creating a private retreat ideal for guests or older children. Further enhancing the property is a self-contained annex, featuring a bedroom, kitchen/living area, and bathroom. This versatile space is perfect for extended family, visitors, independent living, or potential rental income. Throughout the home, the exceptional quality of finish is immediately apparent, with careful attention to detail, contemporary fittings, and a cohesive, high-spec aesthetic. -Location & Lifestyle- Situated in a popular residential area of Whitton, this exceptional home enjoys a convenient and family-friendly setting within easy reach of excellent schools, transport connections, and local amenities. -Schools- The property is ideally positioned for a number of highly regarded primary and secondary schools, making it an excellent choice for families. Well-rated options nearby include Chase Bridge Primary School, Nelson Primary School, and Waldegrave School, with additional reputable secondary and sixth-form options within the wider Twickenham and Richmond borough. 5min walking distance from Kneller Hall School, an upcoming independent secondary school in Twickenham, part of the Dukes Education group, set to open in September 2027 -Transport Links- Whitton and Twickenham railway stations are both easily accessible, providing direct services into London Waterloo, making this an ideal location for commuters. The area is also well served by local bus routes and offers convenient road access to the A316, M3, and wider motorway network, connecting to Central London, Heathrow Airport, Richmond, and Kingston. The location offers excellent connectivity into Central London and surrounding areas. -Local Amenities & Recreation- The property is within easy reach of Whitton High Street, offering a variety of supermarkets, independent shops, cafÃ©s, and everyday conveniences. Nearby Twickenham town centre provides a wider selection of restaurants, boutiques, and leisure facilities. For outdoor recreation, residents can enjoy nearby parks and green spaces, including Murray Park, just 2min waking distance, Crane Park and Kneller Gardens, as well as riverside walks along the Thames in nearby Richmond and Twickenham. The area also offers excellent sports and leisure facilities, including gyms, rugby clubs, and golf courses. This is a rare opportunity to acquire a spacious, beautifully finished home offering versatility, comfort, and modern style in equal measure. address: St Leonards Road, East Sheen SW14
description: Description A superbly located four-bedroom Victorian home offering a wealth of period and original features in a highly desirable setting. The property showcases high ceilings, sash windows, fireplaces, stained glass, and ceiling roses, with a light-filled, airy interior and generously proportioned rooms. The ground floor comprises a welcoming entrance hall with storage, spacious bay-fronted lounge, a dining room, and a superb open-plan kitchen/breakfast room with French doors opening to the south-facing garden.The First floor offers a landing, bay-fronted master bedroom, two further double bedrooms, and a refurbished modern bathroom. Stairs lead up to the loft conversion offering a good size double bedroom with ensuite and eaves storage space. Externally, there is a walled front garden and a substantial south-facing rear garden featuring a patio, lawn, established planting and mature trees, with practical rear access via a gate. This home combines timeless period character with bright, contemporary living in a premier location close to the outstanding Thompson House Primary School, offering a superb balance of charm, space and lifestyle. The location Located at the heart of East Sheen, St Leonards offers an exceptional opportunity to live in a highly sought-after, family-oriented neighbourhood renowned for its vibrant community spirit and outstanding local amenities. This charming home is perfectly positioned on the doorstep of a variety of local shops, restaurants, and the bustling town centre, providing the best of both worlds, tranquility and convenience. The area boasts an abundance of outdoor spaces, including the stunning Richmond Park, Palewell Common, and Sheen Common, ideal for leisurely walks, picnics, outdoor activities, and enjoying nature. The nearby River Thames further enhances the outdoor lifestyle, offering scenic walks. The local community is friendly and active, with a lively, outdoor-oriented culture that appeals to families, professionals, and outdoor enthusiasts alike. A major highlight is the property's proximity to the renowned ofsted rated 'Outstanding' Thomson House Primary School, located just a short walk away, making it an ideal location for families seeking excellent education. The area also offers a host of outstanding secondary schools and nurseries, all within easy reach. Commuters benefit from quick access to Mortlake Station, providing convenient links to central London, while the vibrant town centre offers a diverse array of shops, cafes, and leisure options along White Hart Lane and in Barnes Village. The neighbourhood's active lifestyle is complemented by an array of local amenities, including boutique shops, cafes, and plenty of outdoor pursuits, making it a lively yet welcoming community for residents of all ages. This property combines the tranquility of a close-knit community with the vibrancy of a lively, outdoor-focused lifestyle, truly an outstanding place to call home. With easy access to beautiful green spaces, excellent schools, and excellent transport links, it offers the perfect balance of family- friendly comfort and urban convenience in one of London's most desirable areas. Ivy Gate are delighted to introduce to the market this substantial and thoughtfully extended terraced home, offering well-balanced accommodation arranged over three floors. The ground floor has been cleverly opened to create a flowing, family-focused layout. A welcoming hallway leads through to a spacious dining room, which in turn opens into a bright reception area overlooking the garden. The proportions here are excellent, making it a genuine family living and entertaining space with the added bonus of under floor heating to the ground floor. To the rear sits a well-appointed kitchen, fitted with modern cabinetry, integrated appliances, and ample worktop space. The layout works exactly as you would hope for modern life- space for a large dining table and easy day-to-day practicality, and Bi-fold doors open directly onto a raised deck, seamlessly connecting indoor and outdoor living during the warmer months. The first floor provides three well-proportioned bedrooms. The principal bedroom is a particularly generous double, complemented by two further bedrooms that are ideal for children, guests, or home working. A contemporary family bathroom serves this level. The loft has been converted to create an impressive fourth bedroom suite. With good ceiling height, eaves storage and its own shower room, this floor works perfectly as a main bedroom retreat, guest suite, or teenage space. The rear garden extends to approximately 50 ft in length and is thoughtfully landscaped, with a raised decked seating area, leading onto the lawn and established planting borders and a collection of mature olive trees. At the rear, the property enjoys a wonderful open aspect backing onto green space and a recreation ground and childrens playground, giving a far more open feel than many terraced homes. Alexandra Road is widely regarded as one of Thames Dittonâs premier residential roads, combining a strong community feel with excellent convenience. The A3 is nearby, giving straightforward access to central London and the M25. The area is particularly popular with families thanks to an excellent choice of both state and independent schools. Well-regarded options nearby include Thames Ditton Infant School, Thames Ditton Junior School, and Hinchley Wood School, along with several respected independent schools in the surrounding area. Description A charming and superbly presented three-bedroom end-of-terrace Victorian cottage, thoughtfully extended and remodelled to create a stunning family home. address: Heywoods, 11 Ashley Road, Thames Ditton, Surrey KT7
description: A distinguished Edwardian semi detached home in one of Thames Ditton's most sought-after locations. Description A distinguished Edwardian residence (circa 1902), brought to market for the first time in over 50 years, this elegant four bedroom semi detached home offers a rare opportunity to secure a characterful period property in the heart of Thames Ditton. Set across three floors with tall ceilings, generous proportions and abundant natural light, it retains beautiful original features including stained glass windows and a working fireplace. The welcoming entrance hall leads to a bright front reception room with an original fireplace, complemented by a spacious dining room overlooking the garden. At the rear, the kitchen flows into a generous breakfast room and on to a light filled garden room that connects the house to the outdoors. A standout feature is the dry, well ventilated wine cellar with hipo pump; an uncommon and valuable asset for homes of this era. The first floor offers three well proportioned bedrooms, including a wide principal bedroom and a further double room with garden views. A versatile fourth bedroom and family bathroom complete this level. The top floor provides a large additional double bedroom, ideal for guests or home working, along with further loft storage and a second bathroom. The rear garden offers a peaceful retreat with mature planting, and the propertyâs attractive period frontage enhances its kerb appeal. Cherished by one family for half a century, this exceptional Edwardian home combines original character with exciting potential for sympathetic modernisation; an increasingly rare opportunity in one of Thames Dittonâs most desirable locations. Location Ashley Road is located in the heart of Thames Ditton, a charming village ideally placed for commuting with Thames Ditton Station just 0.3 miles away providing regular and direct trains into London Waterloo in around 35 minutes. The local shops and amenities of the village itself are just around the corner, with more extensive shopping available at Surbiton (1.5 miles) and the major shopping centre of Kingston upon Thames (2 miles). There are also excellent local bus services. Comprehensive leisure and recreational opportunities are available in the local area, including parks, golf courses, tennis clubs, racing at Sandown, cinemas and a wide choice of restaurants and bars. The world-famous Hampton Court Palace is just 1.4 miles away as is Bushy Park and the River Thames. The A3 is approximately 3 miles away providing good access to London, the motorway network and Gatwick and Heathrow airports. The area is very popular with families and there is an excellent selection of schools available both in the state and private sector including Hinchley Wood Senior school and Thames Ditton Infants and Junior school. Square Footage: 1,807 sq f

address: Tudor House, Hampton Court Road, East Molesey, Surrey KT8
description: Characterful Grade II listed home backing onto Bushy Park. Description Set behind a gated wall, Tudor House is a delightful period home dating back to the late 1600s and reputedly designed by an assistant to famous architect Sir Christopher Wren and built for horticulturalist Abraham Fish who was the gardener in chief for joint monarchs William and Mary. The property which has been recently renovated by the owners, enjoys a picturesque courtyard garden to the front with an array of plants and shrubbery; the perfect place for al fresco dining. Welcoming you into this delightful home is the dining area featuring a beautiful stone floor, feature fireplace and bay window. Adjacent is the kitchen which offers a good range of dark blue wall and base mounted cabinetry, granite worktops and integrated appliances. From the dining area, a wooden staircase leads up to the first floor from where stunning views of Hampton Court Palace and the Royal gardens can be enjoyed. This floor comprises a cosy living room as well as bedroom two which offers flexibility for use as a study featuring wood panelled walls and a feature fireplace. The stairs continue to the second floor which similarly enjoys the views over the Palace and grounds. There are two bedrooms on this floor and a well-appointed bathroom with bath and separate shower. Location Tudor House is situated in a fantastic position backing Bushy Park and enjoying magnificent views of the Royal gardens of Hampton Court Palace opposite. The River Thames is also close by providing scenic towpath walks. Hampton Court Station is 0.6 miles away and provides regular and direct trains into London Waterloo from 33 minutes. Bridge Road, located in popular 'Hampton Court Village', is 0.8 miles away and offers an array of restaurants, bars, boutiques and shops. More extensive shopping can be found in nearby Kingston upon Thames (1.4 miles). The road links are excellent with the M3 being just 4 miles away which links with the M25 and M4 and London Heathrow airport is located about 10 miles away. Square Footage: 1,217 sq f

property_name: 4 bed end terrace house for sale
description: Ruth Conlon of Keller Williams is delighted to present this beautiful home in an award-winning development, which won the What House Gold Award for Best House 2016. The project ('The Longfords') from Q Developments was created in partnership with 5Plus Architects and was designed to create high-quality, sustainable homes that blend harmoniously with the surrounding neighbourhood. The judges described the homes as 'tardis-like', and praised the well-planned, light-filled living spaces and the seamless flow between them. They highlighted the clever use of timber screens on the front elevations, the striking roof light above the stairs, and the thoughtful colour palette throughout. Nestled within a small, gated mews of three homes, this stunning end-of-terrace contemporary four-bedroom house offers a tranquil retreat with a garden, terraces, off-street parking, and secure entry behind a gatehouse. The gated development, with CCTV, provides peace of mind, making it ideal for frequent travellers or those seeking a secure environment. Entering the home, the light-filled hallway feels spacious and includes a large cloakroom. It leads into an open-plan kitchen/dining/reception room that flows effortlessly into the garden and terrace. The kitchen, designed to be the heart of the home, boasts modern fittings, including two large fridge freezers, two ovens, and a multi-functional tap with instant hot and filtered water. Whether youâre enjoying the breakfast bar or relaxing by the bio-ethanol fireplace, this space is perfect for everyday living. A front section of the room could serve as a cosy reading corner or even accommodate a dining table or piano, as it once did for the current owner. Downstairs, the oak staircase leads to a versatile family room or guest suite with fitted wardrobes, opening onto a second terrace connected to the main garden. This space offers endless possibilities-ideal as an office, playroom, games room, or hobby room. The lower level also features a full-sized bathroom, utility room, and generous pantry or storage room. It could even function as independent living quarters, with its own separate side access for added convenience.The space is constructed with waterproof caltite reinforced concrete and includes a secondary cavity drainage system for added durability. On the top floor, the master suite offers a peaceful retreat with a view of the garden, with a small terrace, fitted wardrobes, and an en-suite shower room. A family bathroom, second bedroom, and third single bedroom, currently used as an office, complete this level. The home is equipped with modern technology, including recessed downlights with dimmer switches, sky+ cabling, TV and radio antennae, underfloor heating, and a ventilating heat recovery system. The development also features eco-friendly materials, with slate roofs, green roofs, and masonry elevations incorporating wet-cast stone assemblies. The annual service charge for the mews is approximately Â£850. EPC rating C. The location is perfect for those seeking access to both central London and the airport, with Teddington and Hampton Wick stations nearby. Whether commuting to Heathrow, Waterloo, or King's Cross, you can be there within an hour and now within the 'super-loop express' Heathrow and parts of London are more accessible than they have ever been, via Teddington. For those who enjoy nature, the historical Hampton Court and Bushy Park are just moments away, while Kingston offers excellent shopping options, including the Fenwick Centre. The exclusive Lensbury Club is also nearby, and the area is served by top-rated schools, including Collis, Hampton Wick & St John The Baptist, and secondary schools like Teddington, Grey Court, and Turing House. Teddington High Street is the heart of this charming, riverside town, offering a perfect blend of traditional village appeal and modern convenience. Lined with a variety of independent boutiques, trendy cafes, and high-end restaurants, itâs the perfect place to spend a leisurely afternoon or enjoy a night out. Whether youâre looking for unique homeware, artisanal food, or a refreshing drink in one of the cozy pubs, the High Street caters to all tastes. With its picturesque surroundings, friendly atmosphere, and easy access to the River Thames, Teddington High Street is a vibrant and welcoming hub for both locals and visitors alike. Also just a short walk away is Hampton Wick. A quaint area also offering a selection of independent shops, cafes and traditional pubs, as well as excellent transport links. A quick short cut under Kingston bridge and you are on the banks of the River Thames, offering the perfect setting for a leisurely stroll with your dog or a scenic run along the water. What does the current owner love? Owning this modern home has allowed for a lifestyle of luxury, comfort, and style, with every detail thoughtfully designed. The prime location adds an extra layer of convenience, while the added security ensures peace of mind. What do I love? I was immediately impressed by the tall ceilings and the grand front door, which set the tone for a home that feels both expansive and welcoming. I love the shops and restaurants in Teddington high street, and a family favourite pub is also The Anglers on the riverside. Viewings are highly recommended. Get in touch to book

This charming Edwardian semi-detached home offers a wonderful opportunity to secure a recently redecorated and generously proportioned four-bedroom property in one of Wimbledon's most sought-after areas. Peacefully positioned on a desirable residential road within the popular 'Australia' streets, it is ideally located for easy access to Wimbledon Town Centre, South Wimbledon Tube Station, and an excellent selection of local amenities and transport links. Arranged over three floors, the house provides well-balanced and versatile accommodation throughout. The spacious reception room and adjoining dining area create an ideal setting for both family living and entertaining, while the kitchen opens directly onto the rear garden. Additional features include a dedicated home office with side access and a low-maintenance patio garden. Wimbledon is renowned for its commitment to educational excellence. The area boasts a world-class reputation for its schools in the private and state sector including Holy Trinity CofE Primary School, St Mary's Catholic Primary School, The Priory CofE School, Pelham Primary School, The Priory CofE School, Willington School, The Study, Wimbledon High and Kings College Junior & Senior School offering a top-tier education that attracts students worldwide, making Wimbledon a hub for academic achievement and intellectual growth. Wimbledon offers a quality off family life more akin to the country than London offering easy access to the High Street and Common. address: Tayles Cottage West Street, Epsom KT17
description: Occasionally, a house comes onto the market where words and photos just cannot do it justice and it really needs to be seen in person to be fully appreciated. Tayles Cottage is one of those rare finds. Originally built in the 1840s as the coach house to Tayles Hill House, it was converted in the 1950s into what is now an enchanting four-bedroom residence which blends period charm with contemporary style in perfect harmony. Discreetly tucked away on West Street, Tayles Cottage boasts 10-foot ceilings, elegant, double-glazed hardwood casement windows, and character features such as its exposed timber beams. The generous layout offers superb living space including a bright living room, a formal dining room, a stylish kitchen/breakfast room, utility room, and a ground floor shower room. Upstairs, you'll find four well-proportioned bedrooms (three with built-in wardrobes), a family bathroom, and a cozy study area. The home exudes a sense of tranquil calm throughout-ideal for those seeking a unique, character-filled property with a modern twist. Outside The private, low-maintenance garden, complete with irrigation and lighting systems, originally designed by rhs award-winning landscape gardener Andy Sturgeon, provides a tranquil green retreat. Mature trees and hedging offer privacy and security from the road, while a bespoke wooden play area brings joy for younger residents. A large, detached garage sits conveniently to the side and affords access to the garden and home. Situation Set in the heart of Ewell village, Tayles Cottage is walking distance to Ewell West and Ewell East stations (Zone 6) with direct links into central London. The charming High Street is a short stroll away along Ewell Grove, with a mix of local shops, convenience stores, restaurants, and a popular craft beer and wine bar. Nature lovers will appreciate the proximity to Hogsmill Riverside, Horton Country Park, and Nonsuch Park. The property also falls within the catchment area for many popular primary and secondary schools and Nescot College, all within easy reach. Property Ref Number: Ham-5769

description: An exciting opportunity for significant redevelopment (STPP). This has now lapsed but could be re-instated subject to the necessary consent. The owner has also acquired two small adjacent triangles of land, one of which fronts Palace Road. The owner has informed Savills that, following their own enquiries and subject to the relevant consent, it may be possible to change the property's address to Palace Road once any redevelopment work has been completed at the property and on the basis the front door is repositioned to front Palace Road. On the first floor there are three bedrooms all of which benefit from built-in wardrobes and a family bathroom. Externally the wrap around garden is mainly laid to lawn and is well-screened from the road. To the front of the property the paved driveway provides off-street parking in addition to the garage. Please note this property is available with no onwards chain. address: 4 The Orchard, Meadow Walk, Ewell Village KT17
description: Situated at the end of a private access road and occupying a tucked away corner position, this unique contemporary style detached bungalow offers spacious and well appointed accommodation throughout and benefits from two detached garages and a cabin/home office. This stunning and versatile home is perfect for those who value privacy and seclusion and this detached bungalow has been modernised to a high standard with spacious living accommodation that offers further potential for a large loft conversion (subject to planning) You enter the home via an impressive entrance hall with access to all rooms and a useful utility room and there is a spacious living room which is centred around a contemporary style feature fireplace and a recess for the TV. From here there are wooden bifold doors which lead to what is definitely the heart of this home, the kitchen which is fully fitted in a range of floor and wall mounted units. There is plenty of space for relaxing with the family and entertaining friends with patio doors that lead out to a large sun terrace and the rear garden. There are three generously sized double bedrooms with an ensuite to the master bedroom, all with double glazed windows and conveniently located close to a generously sized bathroom suite. The property benefits from level landscaped front and rear gardens laid mainly to lawn with with a large paved sun terrace and a raised swimming pool with a pool house and detached summer house. To the front is a detached cabin/garden room which can be used as work from home office and a den for the kids or grandkids to play and relax. To the front are two single detached garages which are located opposite each other, both of which provide off road parking. The private road is owned by No 4 and the Freehold can be included in the sale, subject to the agreeing the final sales price. 4 The Orchard is located at the end of a small cul-de sac which is strategically nestled between the charming Ewell village and the bustling Stoneleigh Broadway, ensuring convenient access to an array of shops, amenities, excellent commuting facilities and a plethora of highly regarded schools. The highly desirable Ewell Village has a rich background dating back to the Bronze age and at the end of the middle ages King Henry VIII established Nonsuch Palace (now Nonsuch Park) in 1538. The High Street offers a variety of shops, restaurants, cafÃ©s and pubs. Bourne Hall hosts a public library, subterranean theatre, gymnasium, cafÃ©, and local museum. It regularly holds gatherings such as fayres and exhibitions. In the heart of the village lies the picturesque Hogsmill river leading up to the nature reserve. There are a range of popular local schools and of course both Ewell East and West stations (zone 6) offer easy access to London with Waterloo and Victoria taking approximately 40 minutes. Tenure - Freehold Council tax band -

description: Tudors are pleased to offer for sale this well loved and cared for three bedroom end of terrace home, which is tucked away in a quiet position in a cul de sac within the Bishop fox Way development. Externally there is a private and secluded rear garden with patio and artificial grass areas with pretty pergola area and a side access gate and a door leading from the garden directly into a garage with power/light and up/over door. To the front there is a driveway for off road parking leading to the garage. The front garden has shrub areas along with artificial grass. Other benefits include double-glazing and gas central heating. (EPC rating: D) Elmbridge Borough Council = Band E Located in the pleasant Bishop Fox Development within easy reach of several primary schools, including Hurst Park school, Chandlers field School and St. Albans school which recently had Outstanding Ofsted report along with a newly built secondary school; Heathside. Harmes Turner Brown are delighted to offer this 1930's, four bedroom semi-detached home being offered for sale for the first time in approx 35 years, offering great scope to extend s.t.p.p. And improve. Located down a quiet cul-de-sac in Walton on Thames. The ground floor features two well sized reception areas, wooden kitchen with a great range of eye and base level units, also a downstairs cloakroom with utility section. Upstairs, there are four bedrooms, including a master with an en suite shower room. A second family bathroom serves the remaining bedrooms. Outside, the property has a private driveway and a garage, offering off street parking and storage. The rear garden enjoys a sunny aspect and plenty of space for children to play or for gardening projects. The location is ideal for families - within walking distance of local Walton schools, the River Thames, and the Xcel Leisure Centre. Shops, parks, and transport links are also close by, making this a well-connected and convenient place to live. This is a great opportunity for anyone looking for a well located home with scope to improve and add value. Viewings should be arranged at your earliest convenience and can be done by contacting the vendors sole agent.

property_name: 4 bed link detached house for sale
description: Beautiful four bedroom home moments from West End Green. Description Tucked away within this quiet cul-de-sac, this well-presented family home is one of the largest in the development. Upon entering the property via a covered porch, you are welcomed into a bright hallway complete with hard wood effect flooring which features throughout much of the ground floor. There is a double-length living room with feature fireplace and leads through to the orangery at the rear which can be used as a gym, study or playroom. In the centre of the ground floor is a formal dining area with views across the rear garden. The L-shaped kitchen provides space for a breakfast area along with a range of floor and wall mounted cabinets, Butler sink and freestanding Mercury Range Cooker. The ground floor is completed by a study, guest WC and single garage. To the first floor are four double bedrooms. The principal bedroom is situated at the front and benefits from a dressing room and ensuite bathroom with separate bath and shower. The three other bedrooms all share use of a modern family bathroom. Externally, the garden wraps around the property creating a number of different spaces to utilise and enjoy. There is a lovely paved terrace which is west-facing, perfect for outdoor dining and entertaining. The rest of the garden has a mix of lawn, borders and mature shrubbery whilst there is space to create a vegetable patch should you wish. To the front there is off-street parking for two cars along with an integral garage. Please note that photographs are more than six months old. Location This property is located in the picturesque 'West End Village' area of Esher with its idyllic duck pond and cricket green and close to the very popular Garsons Farm. Esher High Street is around 1 mile away offering an array of bars, restaurants, shops and boutiques, Everyman cinema and Waitrose supermarket. The A3 and M25 are easily accessible providing routes to central London, Heathrow and Gatwick airports and both Esher and Hersham train stations are under two miles away providing regular and direct trains into London Waterloo. The Elmbridge Borough is popular with families as it offers an excellent range of both state and independent schools. Square Footage: 1,962 sq ft Additional Info Service charge Â£144 per annu

description: Charming Georgian Cottage with Secret Garden and Historic Character in the Heart of Petersham. Nestled on the picturesque Petersham Road and steeped in local history, this enchanting three-bedroom Georgian cottage offers a truly rare opportunity to own a beautifully preserved slice of old Richmond. Featured in the acclaimed "A Glimpse of Old Ham & Petersham" , this home blends period elegance with thoughtful modern updates, retaining all its original charm while offering practical upgrades for comfortable 21st-century living. Step through the door and you're instantly greeted by warmth and character. The entrance hallway is laid with striking black-and-cream chequered ceramic tiles, leading to a generous reception and dining area with newly installed oak parquet flooring, exposed sodablasted timber beams, and cast iron radiators throughout, white in the bedrooms, pewter in the living areas, and hand-polished in the hall. The living space flows seamlessly through French doors onto a tranquil courtyard, perfect for evening drinks or summer brunches. The handmade kitchen is well-equipped with quality appliances including a Neff oven and hob, Miele dishwasher, and an American-style fridge freezer with water and ice dispenser. Upstairs, three well-proportioned bedrooms await, all recently re-carpeted with a newly laid sisal runner on the stairs. Two of the rooms are comfortable doubles, both accommodating king-sized beds, while the third makes for an ideal nursery or home office. Across a quiet lane behind the cottage is the true surprise, an allotment-style garden measuring approx. 50ft x 25ft, lovingly landscaped with Jacksons fencing, granite cobbles, an entertaining/dining area, and a converted home office shed. It feels wonderfully private, like discovering your own hidden garden through an arched gate. description: Description Property Reference Number: 101777. Fully Renovated 4-Bedroom Detached Bungalow on a Prominent Corner Plot in a Desirable Worcester Park Cul-de-Sac Key Highlights â¢ Recently refurbished throughout to a high standard â¢ Impressive detached bungalow occupying a generous corner position â¢ Four well-proportioned bedrooms â¢ Multiple reception areas including conservatory â¢ Modern kitchen with dining space overlooking the garden â¢ Beautiful private rear garden with entertaining areas â¢ Double garage and extensive driveway parking â¢ Excellent commuter links to Central London â¢ Further potential for extension (STPP) Situated in a peaceful and highly regarded cul-de-sac in Worcester Park, this impressive detached bungalow has recently undergone extensive renovation, creating a beautifully updated home that combines generous living space with modern comfort. Set on a substantial corner plot, the property offers approximately 214 sq m of accommodation, landscaped gardens, and significant potential for future development subject to planning permission. Upon entering the property, a welcoming hallway leads to a bright and spacious reception room, ideal for both relaxing and entertaining. The layout has been thoughtfully designed to provide flexible living arrangements suitable for families, professionals, or those seeking single-level living without compromising on space. The home features four generously sized bedrooms, all offering excellent natural light and comfortable proportions. One of the bedrooms enjoys attractive views over the rear garden and could easily serve as a home office or guest suite. The heart of the property is the modern kitchen and dining area, which overlooks the garden and connects seamlessly to additional living and dining spaces. A conservatory at the rear further enhances the sense of light and space, providing a tranquil setting with direct access to the garden. Outside, the private rear garden has been carefully landscaped, offering a combination of lawn and seating areas ideal for outdoor entertaining. A charming summer house and dedicated barbecue area provide a wonderful space for gatherings during the warmer months. To the front, the property benefits from a large driveway and double garage, offering ample off-street parking for several vehicles and excellent storage options. The location is particularly convenient, being within easy reach of Worcester Park High Street and the mainline railway station, providing regular services to London Waterloo in under 30 minutes. The area also benefits from good road connections including the A3 and M25, as well as nearby access to Underground services at Morden. A selection of well-regarded local schools are close by, including Malden Parochial Primary School, The Mead Primary School and Cuddington Croft Primary School, making the property particularly appealing to families. Planning permission was previously granted in April 2023 for a significant first-floor extension incorporating four additional bedrooms, presenting exciting future possibilities for buyers wishing to expand the property further (subject to the relevant consents). This beautifully refurbished home presents a rare opportunity to acquire a substantial detached bungalow in one of Worcester Parkâs most desirable residential settings. Early viewing is highly recommended. Contact us today to arrange your appointment. Getting Around: Location & Connectivity Worcester Park is a well-connected and highly convenient location for both commuters and families. The nearby Worcester Park mainline station provides regular rail services into London Waterloo, allowing straightforward access to Central London. The area also benefits from excellent road connections, with the A3 and M25 easily accessible, making travel to neighbouring towns and further afield particularly convenient. Popular destinations such as Kingston upon Thames and Epsom are just a short drive away and offer an extensive range of shopping, dining, and leisure facilities. In addition, a number of local bus routes operate throughout the area, linking Worcester Park with surrounding districts and nearby transport hubs, providing further flexibility for daily travel. Local Life: Worcester Park offers a lively and well-established community with a wide range of everyday amenities close at hand. The High Street features a selection of cafÃ©s, restaurants, and local shops, providing plenty of options for dining, socialising, and daily convenience. For more extensive retail and leisure opportunities, Kingston upon Thames is located nearby, home to a variety of major retailers, independent stores, restaurants, and entertainment venues, including the popular Bentall Centre. The surrounding area also offers a number of parks, recreational facilities, and cultural attractions, creating an appealing balance between suburban living and easy access to vibrant town centres. With its friendly atmosphere, strong local amenities, and excellent connectivity, Worcester Park continues to be a highly sought-after location for buyers looking for both convenience and quality of life

A detached three bedroom family home located on a popular residential road, close to Marney's Village Inn, duck pond and Esher common. This traditional family home retains architectural features of the art-deco period together with updated 'charcoal grey' double glazed windows fitted to a Crittall style across the front elevation to preserve its original style and appearance. The property is arranged naturally over two floors, is extended at ground floor level and benefits from a beautifully established rear garden of abt. 80ft, a lawned front garden with off-street-parking and a side driveway leading to a detached garage. The accommodation is already well balanced but there is plenty of scope to extend, adapt or re-model if desired. This comfortable family home is well presented throughout and consists of an attractive recessed storm porch with panelled front door completed with stained and leaded glazing with matching side windows. This leads to a good size entrance hall with under stair coats cupboard and useful guest W.C. There are two very nice interconnecting reception rooms with ornamental fireplaces, leading through to an extended kitchen / diner with natural wood flooring, which looks over and provides access to the garden via double doors. To complete the ground floor there is an excellent utility area with superb storage solutions, and an external door opening to side access. Upstairs, you will find a return staircase with half-landing window, three double bedrooms; the principle and second bedrooms both feature bay windows and built-in wardrobes, whilst the third bedroom features a pretty corner window. All bedrooms are supported by a good size main family bathroom fitted with a modern suite, complete with double ended bath and separate shower. Externally, the gardens are established with a variety of plants, trees and shrubs with lawns front and rear, and both sunny and shaded areas in the back garden to enjoy the natural sun light. There is a separate tool shed behind the garage. Location: Elm Tree Avenue is a sought after residential road within close proximity of mainline stations (Esher, 0.8 miles) and (Thames Ditton, 0.6 miles) providing direct trains to London Waterloo in around 23 and 31 minutes respectively. By road, the A3 is under 2 miles away, providing access to central London, the M25 and the motorway network. Esher High Street is only 1.4 miles away, there you will find an Everyman cinema, several coffee shops, a Waitrose supermarket, as well as a great range of restaurants and bars. The local shops and amenities of both Thames Ditton village (1.4 miles) and Hampton Court Village (1.6 miles) are also accessible. More extensive shopping is found in nearby Kingston upon Thames (3.3 miles). The world famous Hampton Court Palace and grounds plays host to annual cultural events, concerts and an outdoor cinema, with royal parks (Bushy & Home) for extensive outdoor activities, including beautiful riverside walks along the banks the River Thames. The Elmbridge area is popular with families and offers an excellent choice of state and independent schools

address: 2 Giggs Hill Villas, Portsmouth Road, Thames Ditton KT7
description: Beautifully refurbished Victorian home with views over Giggs Hill Green. Description Set just off of Giggs Hill Green and conveniently placed for Thames Ditton Village is this beautifully presented and stylish Victorian home. As you enter this charming home you are welcomed by a collection of Victorian features such as high ceilings and pretty coving and cornicing. To the right is the beautifully finished and contemporary reception room with marble surround feature fireplace and fitted cabinetry on either side. There is underfloor heating and wood effect ceramic tiles throughout the entire ground floor. Through to the left is a bespoke Italian fitted kitchen with calacatta gold quartz worktop, Fischer and Paykel fridge freezer, aed ovens, Neff induction hob & dishwasher, wine fridge and Quooker tap which provides hot, sparkling and filtered water. There is a breakfast bar with seating and plenty of storage built in. Crittall style doors lead from the kitchen to the garden, ideal for entertaining. The guest WC has a beautiful marble sink, and Buster & Punch fittings complete this floor. Upstairs there are three bedrooms. The principal bedroom has fitted wardrobes and triple glazing with special sound proof glass filled with Argon gas. The second bedroom also has fitted wardrobes, and the third bedroom is currently used as a study. The bathroom is modern and sleek and has an over-bath shower. Outside, there is a private and secluded patio garden with wooden panelling, lighting and useful side access. Location Thames Ditton is an attractive Surrey village offering both charm and country character. There are local shops and amenities in the village itself and nearby Surbiton, Kingston and Esher cater for everyday necessities. The local shops and amenities of the village itself are just 0.3 miles from the property and include an array of shops, pubs and cafÃ©s. This area is popular with families as there are many highly regarded state and private schools for all ages. Commuting links are excellent with Surbiton station providing a fast and frequent service to London Waterloo as well as Thames Dittonâs own station (trains from 17 and 31 minutes respectively). The A3, M3 and M25 are all easily accessible along with Heathrow and Gatwick Airports. Thames Ditton is 17 miles South West to Central London. Square Footage: 1,148 sq ft Additional Info The property runs off mains water, electricity, gas and drainage. Council Tax Band E, Elmbridge Borough Council
